_meta.description: Smoke benchmark pack — 3 companies × 5 personas × 2 presets × 1 slider = 30 emails

seller.current_product: Trademark Search, Screening, and Brand Protection
seller.offer_lock: Trademark Search, Screening, and Brand Protection
seller.cta_offer_lock: Open to a quick 15-minute chat next week?
seller.company_notes: Corsearch is the global leader in trademark clearance, brand protection, and IP enforcement. Trusted by over 5,000 brands across pharma, FMCG, tech, and retail. We help IP teams eliminate risk from similar marks before filing, monitor live infringement, and act on counterfeits at scale.

companies.name: Acme Consumer Brands
companies.company_notes: Acme Consumer Brands is a mid-size FMCG company headquartered in Chicago with a portfolio of household cleaning, personal care, and food products sold across North America and Europe. They filed 12 trademark applications in 2024 across new SKU lines and recently flagged two potential conflicts in their European filing backlog. Their IP team is small (3 FTEs) and relies on outside counsel for screening.

personas.name: Sarah Chen
personas.title: CEO
personas.research_text: Sarah Chen joined Acme Consumer Brands as CEO in 2022 after a decade at a Big 4 FMCG conglomerate. She has been focused on international expansion and new product category launches. Under her leadership the company entered the European market and filed trademarks in 6 new categories. Brand integrity and risk management are recurring themes in her public statements and earnings calls. She has spoken at industry events about protecting brand equity in international markets.

personas.name: Marcus Williams
personas.title: VP Legal / IP Counsel
personas.research_text: Marcus Williams leads the legal and IP function at Acme Consumer Brands. He joined in 2021 from outside counsel where he specialized in trademark prosecution and brand licensing. He is responsible for trademark portfolio management across all product lines and regions. He has been handling the European filing backlog personally with support from two paralegals. He has flagged resource constraints in trademark clearance as a growing risk given the pace of new product launches.

personas.name: Zoe Martinez
personas.title: Head of Brand Protection
personas.research_text: Zoe Martinez manages brand integrity and anti-counterfeiting programs at Acme Consumer Brands. She reports to Marcus Williams and coordinates with supply chain, sales, and marketing on enforcement actions. She recently oversaw a takedown campaign on two e-commerce platforms where Acme products were being counterfeited. She uses a mix of manual monitoring and a legacy tool that has not been updated in two years. She has been evaluating newer platforms that provide automated detection and bulk takedown capabilities.

personas.name: David Reyes
personas.title: Director of Information Security
personas.research_text: David Reyes is responsible for cybersecurity, data governance, and digital risk at Acme Consumer Brands. He recently expanded his remit to include digital brand risk after an incident involving a spoofed domain mimicking an Acme product site. He is working with legal and marketing to define a process for monitoring domain abuse and brand impersonation. He has budget approval authority for new security tooling and has been briefed on the trademark side of the brand protection problem by Marcus Williams.

personas.name: Jordan Lee
personas.title: Senior Program Manager, Operations
personas.research_text: Jordan Lee is a program manager in the operations division at Acme Consumer Brands. They are responsible for cross-functional project delivery including the ERP migration and the new product launch process. They have recently been looped into a project to streamline the trademark clearance workflow after the legal team flagged delays impacting launch timelines. Jordan has procurement authority for software tools under $100K annually and is coordinating vendor evaluations for the IP workflow improvement initiative.

companies.name: Meridian Health Sciences
companies.company_notes: Meridian Health Sciences is a mid-stage biotech and specialty pharma company with a pipeline of 8 compounds in clinical development. They have an active trademark filing program tied to brand names for upcoming commercial launches. Their IP counsel has flagged that trademark screening is a bottleneck ahead of two anticipated NDA submissions. They have experienced two naming conflicts in the past 18 months requiring costly rebrand or licensing.

personas.name: James Okafor
personas.title: CEO
personas.research_text: James Okafor has led Meridian Health Sciences since 2020 and has taken the company from Series B to pre-commercial stage. He has a background in pharma commercialization from his time at two large pharmaceutical companies. He has publicly discussed the cost and reputational impact of late-stage trademark conflicts in drug naming, noting that one recent incident cost the company seven figures and delayed their brand launch by three months. He is focused on de-risking the commercialization path for their lead asset.

personas.name: Priya Patel
personas.title: VP Legal and General Counsel
personas.research_text: Priya Patel oversees legal strategy, IP, and regulatory affairs at Meridian Health Sciences. She manages a portfolio of 40+ trademark filings across the US, EU, and key Asian markets. She has been working with an outside law firm for trademark screening but is frustrated by slow turnaround times and high per-search costs. She is evaluating bringing more of the initial screening in-house to speed up naming decisions early in the development cycle.

personas.name: Aisha Johnson
personas.title: Head of Brand Integrity
personas.research_text: Aisha Johnson was recently promoted to lead brand integrity at Meridian Health Sciences, a newly created role. Her background is in pharmaceutical marketing and brand strategy. She is building out the brand protection function from scratch, focused initially on protecting the commercial brand names for their two pipeline assets expected to reach market in 2026. She is actively evaluating technology platforms for trademark watch services and online infringement monitoring.

personas.name: Natasha Singh
personas.title: Director of Digital and Information Security
personas.research_text: Natasha Singh leads digital security at Meridian Health Sciences. She has recently been pulled into a cross-functional initiative to address brand impersonation risks including fake pharmacy sites claiming to sell Meridian products ahead of commercial launch. She is working with legal to define the scope of digital brand monitoring and has been briefed on the intersection between cybersecurity threat vectors and trademark enforcement. She has experience with third-party brand monitoring tools in a previous role at a larger pharma.

personas.name: Maya Thompson
personas.title: VP Commercial Operations
personas.research_text: Maya Thompson oversees commercial operations at Meridian Health Sciences, including launch readiness programs for their pipeline assets. She is coordinating the brand naming process for their lead compound across regulatory, legal, and marketing stakeholders. She has flagged trademark clearance speed as a critical path dependency for the 2026 launch and is sponsoring a vendor evaluation for an in-house screening solution. She controls the commercial operations budget and has authority to approve new tooling spend.

companies.name: Cascade Commerce Group
companies.company_notes: Cascade Commerce Group operates an e-commerce marketplace platform serving 8,000+ third-party sellers across home goods, apparel, and consumer electronics. They host branded storefronts for 200+ consumer brands and have been expanding their own private label portfolio. They face growing counterfeiting and trademark infringement challenges on the platform from third-party sellers. They recently launched a brand registry program but lack automated tools for proactive detection and enforcement at scale.

personas.name: Elena Vasquez
personas.title: CEO
personas.research_text: Elena Vasquez co-founded Cascade Commerce Group and has grown it from a regional marketplace to a $400M GMV platform. She has been public about the brand trust challenges in marketplace e-commerce and has testified at an industry panel on counterfeiting in online retail. She is focused on building brand relationships and differentiating Cascade on trust and authentication. She has invested in the brand registry program as a strategic initiative and is evaluating partnerships with brand protection technology companies.

personas.name: Robert Kline
personas.title: VP Legal and Compliance
personas.research_text: Robert Kline joined Cascade Commerce Group to build out the legal and compliance function as the platform scaled. He is managing a growing volume of trademark infringement complaints from brand partners and navigating DMCA and Lanham Act liability exposure. He has implemented a manual notice-and-takedown process but it is overwhelmed by the volume of complaints. He has flagged to the board that without automated tooling, legal exposure scales with the platform. He is the decision-maker for brand protection tooling procurement.

personas.name: Kevin Park
personas.title: Head of Brand Trust and Protection
personas.research_text: Kevin Park leads brand trust and protection at Cascade Commerce Group, reporting to Robert Kline. He manages the brand registry program, seller compliance, and proactive enforcement actions. He is building out automated detection capabilities and has been testing several third-party tools for counterfeit listing detection. He is also responsible for the brand partner relationships that depend on effective IP enforcement on the platform. He needs tools that can scale enforcement across 8,000+ sellers without proportional headcount growth.

personas.name: Chris Huang
personas.title: Director of Platform Security and Trust
personas.research_text: Chris Huang is responsible for platform integrity, fraud prevention, and seller trust at Cascade Commerce Group. His team handles account takeovers, fraudulent listings, and increasingly, intellectual property abuse by sellers. He is working with Kevin Park on a joint initiative to detect and remove trademark-infringing listings at the point of upload rather than after customer complaints. He has an engineering team and is evaluating API-first tools that can integrate into the listing ingestion pipeline for pre-publication screening.

personas.name: Alex Nguyen
personas.title: Senior Program Manager, Brand Partnerships
personas.research_text: Alex Nguyen manages the brand partnership program at Cascade Commerce Group, responsible for onboarding and retaining the 200+ branded storefronts on the platform. They act as the primary contact for brand partners on IP protection concerns and feed those requirements back to the legal and platform teams. They have been tracking growing churn risk among brand partners who are frustrated with the pace of trademark enforcement on the platform. They are co-sponsoring the brand protection tooling evaluation with Robert Kline.
